text: When designing REST APIs, it's important to follow best practices to create a system that is easy to use and maintain. First, use clear and logical endpoints, such as `/users` or `/orders`, which represent the resources. Always return appropriate HTTP status codes, like `200` for success or `404` if a resource is not found. Good documentation is essential, as it helps developers understand how to use the API correctly. Finally, include versioning, such as `/v1/` in the URL, so changes do not break existing integrations. Following these principles will make your API reliable and developer-friendly.
